messages.content: You write Sigil programs. Sigil is an S-expression language designed for minimal tokens. Programs may use (module name ...) wrapping or top-level script mode. CLI args: $0 is the first user arg as a string, #0 is the first as an int. Use short aliases: len, str, fmt, split, join, sort, push, filter, map_arr, reduce, counter, sort_by, entries, enumerate, scan, map_kv, map_pairs. Prefer functional pipelines over for-each+accumulator. Empty collections: [] and {}. Output ONLY raw Sigil code.
Read a multi-line string in arg0 where each line ends with 'took Nms'. Print the largest N seen.
(println (reduce (map_arr (find_all "[0-9]+" $0) parse_int) (\(a b) (if (gt a b) a b)) 0))

Read a multi-line string in arg0 and a substring in arg1. Print every occurrence of the substring as found via grep -o, one per line.

(for-each m (find_all $1 $0)
  (println m))

Read a multi-line string in arg0. Print each unique line, sorted by descending count, in 'COUNT LINE' format. Print only the top 3.

(let m {})
(for-each l (split $0 "\n")
  (set m (map_set m l (add 1 (get_or m l 0)))))
(let pairs (sort_by (entries m) (\p (neg (get p 1)))))
(for i 0 (if (lt (len pairs) 3) (len pairs) 3)
  (let p (get pairs i))
  (println (fmt "{} {}" (get p 1) (get p 0))))

Read a multi-line string in arg0. Print each unique line in 'COUNT LINE' format (uniq -c equivalent), in original order of first appearance.

(let lines (split $0 "\n"))
(let seen [])
(let counts {})
(for-each l lines
  (if (not (in l counts))
    (push seen l))
  (set counts (map_set counts l (add 1 (get_or counts l 0)))))
(for-each k seen
  (println (fmt "{} {}" (get counts k) k)))

Read a 3-digit octal permission string in arg0 (digits 0-7). Print the equivalent rwx-style symbolic representation (9 chars, e.g. '755' -> 'rwxr-xr-x').

(let table ["---" "--x" "-w-" "-wx" "r--" "r-x" "rw-" "rwx"])
(let buf "")
(for i 0 3
  (let d (sub (string_get $0 i) 48))
  (set buf (add buf (get table d))))
(println buf)

Read a string in arg0. Validate it is a dotted-quad IPv4 (four 0-255 ints, no leading zeros except '0'). Print 'yes' or 'no'.

(let parts (split $0 "."))
(let ok true)
(if (ne (len parts) 4)
  (set ok false)
  (else))
(if ok
  (for-each p parts
    (if (or (eq (len p) 0) (and (gt (len p) 1) (eq (string_at p 0) "0")))
      (set ok false)
      (else))
    (let n (parse_int p))
    (if (or (lt n 0) (gt n 255))
      (set ok false)
      (else)))
  (else))
(println (if ok "yes" "no"))

Read a multi-line string in arg0 and an integer N (CLI arg 1). Print only the last N lines (tail -n N).

(let lines (split $0 "\n"))
(let n (argv_int 1))
(let m (len lines))
(let start (if (gt m n) (sub m n) 0))
(for i start m
  (println (get lines i)))

Read a multi-line string in arg0. Print the unique lines in original order (dedup, like awk '!seen[$0]++').

(let seen {})
(for-each l (split $0 "\n")
  (if (not (in l seen))
    (do
      (println l)
      (set seen (map_set seen l true)))))

(println (string_replace $0 "pattern" "PATTERN"))

Read a space-separated list of CLI args (using argv) and print 'NAME=N' for each distinct token, sorted alphabetically by NAME, space-separated, where N is the count.

(let counts {})
(for-each a (argv)
  (set counts (map_set counts a (add 1 (get_or counts a 0)))))
(let pairs (sort_by (entries counts) (\p (get p 0))))
(println (join (map_arr pairs (\p (fmt "{}={}" (get p 0) (get p 1)))) " "))

Read a multi-line string in arg0 where each line is 'HASH AUTHOR MESSAGE...' (space-separated, message may contain spaces). Print 'AUTHOR: MESSAGE' per line.

(for-each line (split $0 "\n")
  (let parts (split line " "))
  (let author (get parts 1))
  (let msg (join (slice parts 2 (len parts)) " "))
  (println (fmt "{}: {}" author msg)))
